title: Aspidium anomalum
taxonomy: Aspidium anomalum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which comprises a large group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other fern species. Aspidium anomalum is further classified within the genus Aspidium, which is a group of extinct or possibly extinct species of ferns. The genus Aspidium is part of the subfamily Aspidioideae, which is a subgroup of the family Polypodiaceae. This classification is subject to revision based on ongoing research and updates in fern taxonomy.

intro: Aspidium anomalum, also known as the anomalous maidenhair fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium anomalum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
